claims.claim: We evaluate the viability and throughput characteristics of speculative decoding in llama.cpp using a Qwen3-32B Q4_K_M target model with a Qwen3-0.6B Q8_0 draft model on an NVIDIA GB10 platform.

evidence_refs.quote: # Speculative Decoding with Qwen3-32B on NVIDIA GB10: Workflow Robustness and Throughput Calibration

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Across 13 smoke-test and calibration cases (1 smoke, 3 baseline, 9 speculative), all runs completed successfully with zero exit codes and no memory errors under a no-swap configuration.

evidence_refs.quote: # Speculative Decoding with Qwen3-32B on NVIDIA GB10: Workflow Robustness and Throughput Calibration

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Decode-only speculative speedups ranged from 1.08× to 2.55× depending on prompt type and draft length, with a mean of 1.37×.

evidence_refs.quote: # Speculative Decoding with Qwen3-32B on NVIDIA GB10: Workflow Robustness and Throughput Calibration

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: The best-performing configuration (draft length 4, draft-p-min 0.8) yielded 1.30× speedup on a short-instruction prompt, 1.29× on a structured-list prompt, and 2.55× on a code/text prompt.

evidence_refs.quote: # Speculative Decoding with Qwen3-32B on NVIDIA GB10: Workflow Robustness and Throughput Calibration

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Acceptance rates were strongly prompt-sensitive: 66% for code/text but only 10–20% for the two prose-oriented prompts.

claims.claim: This work addresses a practical question: can a local NVIDIA GB10 system run a Qwen3-32B target model with a Qwen3-0.6B draft under llama.cpp speculative decoding, and is the resulting workflow robust enough to justify further research investment?
